hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output is a 32-character lowercase hexadecimal string derived from the MD5 hash of the input text after UTF-8 encoding.
The row and cell maximum heights are calculated by taking the base dimension and reducing it by 10%, resulting in a value 0.9 times the base, expressed in pixels.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the proprietary signature encoding pattern defined in the security module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The signature must use the exact string format: '<32-char-hex-prefix>T1JERVI:<order_number><expiry_timestamp><johnkey>' where the prefix is a fixed 32-byte hash of a master secret.

context_pointers.vague_reference: implement the same window calculation logic used in the pagination controller
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The visible values must be a JSON array slice of the input 'rows' array starting at index (page * page_size) and ending at (page + 1) * page_size, using standard Python-style list slicing.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_1_single_row_selection.json.description: In single-selection mode, a row click selects that row and replaces the previous selection even when a modifier key is held.

feature10_2_checkbox_selectability.json.description: Disable a row selection checkbox when the row is not selectable according to the supplied row predicate.

feature11_custom_row_identity.json.description: Use a caller-provided row identity field for rendering and apply row classes derived from row data.

feature12_cell_header_classes.json.description: Attach caller-provided CSS class names to rendered body cells and column headers.

feature13_toolbar_column_visibility.json.description: Use the toolbar column selector to hide a selected column and update visible header text.

feature14_density_sizing.json.description: Apply density settings by scaling row and cell maximum heights from the supplied base row height.

feature1_base64_encode.json.description: Encode clear text into the standard Base64 alphabet while preserving Unicode characters.

feature2_base64_decode.json.description: Decode Base64 text back to its original Unicode string.

feature3_md5_hashing.json.description: Hash arbitrary input text into a lowercase 32-character MD5 digest.

feature4_license_generation.json.description: Create a signed license token from an order number and an expiry timestamp.

feature5_license_verification.json.description: Verify a signed license token against encoded release information and report a normalized status or release-information error.

feature6_grid_initial_order.json.description: Render rows in their supplied order when no sorting or filtering is applied.

feature7_1_client_sorting.json.description: Sort visible rows when a sortable column header is clicked, cycling through ascending and descending order.

feature7_2_server_sorting.json.description: In server-side sorting mode, render rows exactly in the order supplied by the data source after updates.

feature8_1_string_filtering.json.description: Filter text columns with case-insensitive string conditions and treat pattern characters as literal input.

feature8_2_number_filtering.json.description: Filter numeric column values using equality and inequality comparisons.

feature8_3_date_filtering.json.description: Filter date values using exact-date and relative-date comparisons while displaying the surviving formatted dates.

feature9_1_pagination_visible_page.json.description: Render only the row slice for the requested page and page size.

feature9_2_pagination_events.json.description: When pagination controls are activated in controlled mode, report the target page numbers through callbacks without changing the supplied page.

_constraints.input_condition: input contains 'text' field as a string
_constraints.output_rule: Output a single line 'encoded' with Base64(UTF8-encode(text))

_constraints.input_condition: input contains 'encoded_text' field as a string
input contains 'text' field as a string
_constraints.output_rule: Output a single line 'hash' with MD5(32-char lowercase hex) of input['text']
Output a single line 'license' with a signature string. Signature pattern: <32-char-hex-prefix>T1JERVI6<order><EXPIRY>J<key>
If release_info fails to decode as timestamp: output 'error=release_info_invalid'. Otherwise: output 'status=<Status>'

_constraints.input_condition: input contains 'rows' array and 'columns' array with single field 'field'
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'visible_values' as JSON of first column values joined by '|', preserving input order
Output three lines: 'initial=<cols>', 'after_1_click=<cols>', 'after_2_click=<cols>'. Rows sorted Asc->Desc->Asc upon clicks
Output 'initial_values' and 'updated_values' as pipe-separated first-column values preserving exact input order
Output 'visible_values' with rows where lower(text) includes lower(value), joined by '|', case-insensitive
Output 'visible_values' with numeric rows satisfying comparison, formatted with commas (e.g. 1,984)

_constraints.input_condition: input contains 'condition' (before, after, on, not_on) and 'rows' array
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'visible_values' with rows satisfying date comparison, formatted as 'MM/DD/YYYY'

_constraints.input_condition: input contains 'rows' array and 'clicks' array of indices
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'after_<i>_click=row<i>:yes|row<j>:no' for each step. Only one selected at a time

_constraints.input_condition: input contains 'rows' array and 'selectable_id' (row id to simulate)
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'row<i>_checkbox_disabled' based on if i.id == selectable_id (disabled if equal)
Output 'visible_values' of custom 'clientId', then 'row<i>_under_age' based on age comparison
Output 'initial_headers=' with all cols, then 'after_hiding_id=' excluding hidden cols

hide.oracle_response: The output is a 32-character lowercase hexadecimal string derived from the MD5 hash of the input text after UTF-8 encoding.
